_comment: INFRA-330 — actionable lessons seed for the 'infra' domain. Each directive is a real, battle-tested rule drawn from a specific session-history failure mode. The DB cost was paid once (the original incident shipped, often with multi-PR recovery work); the lesson is the residue that lets the next agent skip the failure. Seeded into chump_improvement_targets via `chump --seed-ab-lessons <this-file>` with the `ab_seed:infra` tag, so it can be cleared cleanly between A/B trials. Per the COG-032 prereg this is the pool the lessons-injection harness measures.
_authored_from: session 930a8f49-0000 (2026-05-02), drawing on session-discovered + project-history failure modes

directives.directive: When a doc or task instruction references docs/gaps.yaml directly, do NOT edit that file. Per INFRA-188 (2026-05-02) the canonical layout is per-file docs/gaps/<ID>.yaml mirrors of .chump/state.db. The monolithic docs/gaps.yaml was deleted; stale chump binaries occasionally re-create it as a side effect (gitignored). Mutate gaps via chump gap subcommands (reserve/claim/set/ship). Hand-editing the per-file YAMLs trips the pre-commit raw-YAML-edit guard (INFRA-200, blocking).

Edit src/* and config files only inside an isolated worktree under /tmp/<branch> (or .chump/worktrees/<name>), never in the main repo root /Users/jeffadkins/Projects/Chump. Background mutators (sibling agents, dispatch scripts) silently switch the main worktree's branch and stomp uncommitted edits. META-011 (2026-05-01) lost two src/main.rs edits in one session before the rule was adopted. Fast path: git worktree add /tmp/chump-<task> -b <branch> origin/main, work there, ship from there.

Do NOT use git stash without an immediately-paired git stash pop. Named stashes (claude-watch-stash-<N> pattern) without pop accumulate and absorb untracked files indefinitely. META-016 (2026-05-02) discovered this when 4 .chump/notes/* files vanished mid-session into stashes that were never popped. If you need to clear the worktree before a pull: prefer `git checkout -- <files>` for tracked, or commit before pull. If stash is genuinely needed, bracket it: `git stash push -m 'pop-me' && <op> && git stash pop`.

When chump gap commands hang with no output and `ps` shows accumulating processes in state UE, the binary is wedged at _dyld_start due to macOS Sequoia syspolicyd inode-pending-decision state (INFRA-275, 2026-05-02). Direct sqlite3 queries against .chump/state.db work instantly — proof the DB is fine and the binary is the bottleneck. Heal: run scripts/dev/chump-binary-unwedge.sh — it replaces the wedged inode with a fresh copy. DO NOT fall back to writing docs/gaps/<ID>.yaml directly: concurrent siblings each scanning the filesystem for 'next free ID' produce silent collisions (INFRA-301).

Before any `chump gap …` operation, verify binary freshness: `ls -la $(which chump)` mtime vs `git log src/gap_store.rs src/main.rs --since=<timestamp> --oneline`. If main has commits to those paths after your binary's mtime, rebuild before invoking the CLI: `cargo build --release --bin chump && cp target/release/chump ~/.cargo/bin/chump`. INFRA-148 saw stale-binary symptoms 4× in one cycle: chump gap import rejecting valid YAML, --closed-pr flag missing, dump dropping rows, silent ID collisions. Rebuild is cheaper than the friction it prevents.

When a chump CLI invocation hangs OR exits non-zero in a coordination script (gap-reserve.sh, gap-claim.sh, bot-merge.sh), fail loud and STOP. Do NOT silently fall back to filesystem scans for 'next free ID' or any other YAML-write workaround. INFRA-301 (2026-05-02) was filed because trace logs caught a sibling running `for n in 301 302 303; do [ -f docs/gaps/INFRA-$n.yaml ] || ...; done` after chump hung — concurrent siblings each picking the same ID is a silent-collision hazard. Run scripts/dev/chump-binary-unwedge.sh; if still hung, file a gap and stop the cycle.

Cold cargo builds in fresh worktrees take 5–15 minutes (INFRA-202, 2026-05-02). Run scripts/setup/install-sccache.sh once per machine (homebrew install + writes .cargo/config.toml with rustc-wrapper = sccache). First worktree to build a given crate version populates the cache; every subsequent worktree gets <60s rebuilds. .cargo/config.toml is gitignored so CI runners without sccache don't break. Symptom this fixes: bot-merge.sh hitting 900s clippy timeouts on freshly-created worktrees.

Use scripts/coord/chump-commit.sh <files> -m 'msg' instead of `git add && git commit`. The wrapper unstages unrelated files staged by OTHER agents before committing — without it, sibling agent WIP can leak into your commit. Observed twice on 2026-04-17 (memory_db.rs stomp in cf79287, DOGFOOD_RELIABILITY_GAPS.md stomp in a5b5053). The wrapper also runs the same pre-commit hooks but prevents cross-agent staging drift.

Once scripts/coord/bot-merge.sh runs and arms auto-merge, the PR is FROZEN. Pushing more commits to that branch reintroduces the squash-loss footgun PR #52 historically lost 11 commits to (GitHub captures the branch at first-CI-green and drops anything pushed after). If you need to add work, open a NEW PR from a fresh worktree — cheap with the dispatcher — and let the queue land them in order. The merge queue closes the race only if you stop pushing.

When the branch name contains a closed gap ID (e.g. cleanup PRs, revert PRs, post-mortem PRs), the pre-push gap-preflight hook false-positives because it sees the closed-gap ID as a stolen claim. Bypass with: `CHUMP_GAP_CHECK=0 git push origin <branch>`. This bypass is documented in CLAUDE.md and safe — the canonical authority is .chump/state.db, which already shows the gap as done. Do NOT use --no-verify; that bypasses ALL pre-commit/pre-push guards including the load-bearing ones.

Initial gap descriptions consistently underestimate scope by ~30%+ (META-013, 2026-04-30). When a gap touches src/discord.rs, src/gap_store.rs, src/main.rs, or any Rust file >1000 lines, expect quasi-god-module entanglement: 16-call-site refactors, hidden coupling, scope-expansion-on-contact. Stay tightly scoped — only modify the function the gap names. If you discover the change requires touching 5+ files, file a follow-up gap and ship the original change behind a runtime flag rather than expanding scope.

If you need to add fields to an existing gap (acceptance_criteria, depends_on, notes), use `chump gap set <ID> --acceptance-criteria 'a|b|c' --depends-on 'X,Y'` — never hand-edit docs/gaps/<ID>.yaml. The pre-commit raw-YAML-edit guard (INFRA-200, blocking since 2026-05-02) blocks the commit. The CHUMP_RAW_YAML_EDIT=1 bypass + RAW_YAML_REASON: trailer is ONLY for filing-PR bootstrap commits where the row IS the new addition (the chump CLI can't ship-time write a row that doesn't exist yet).

When working in src/reflection_db.rs, src/system_prompt.rs, or any file under the lessons/reflection injection path, do NOT change the CHUMP_LESSONS_AT_SPAWN_N default value. COG-024 (2026-04-30) explicitly defaulted spawn-time lessons OFF until COG-032's A/B harness measures whether lessons help or add noise. The COG-032 prereg (#918, 2026-05-02) is the contract that locks the conditions for flipping that default. Premature flips invalidate the study.

Always run `cargo fmt --all` before pushing any src/*.rs change. CI fast-checks runs `cargo fmt --all -- --check`; missing fmt fails the run, blocks auto-merge, and forces a disable-auto + push-fix + re-arm cycle (~10-15 min recovery per occurrence). Observed 2026-05-03 in PRs #975 and #981 within minutes of each other.

When using `git -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null` (e.g. to bypass the chump-binary-mtime CHUMP_CHECK_BUILD guard), you ALSO bypass the cargo-fmt pre-commit guard. Run `cargo fmt --all` manually before commit when using this bypass.
